security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote message may be altered in transit.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not protect against tampering of vote messages.

assumptions: The communication channel between the user and the contract is insecure.
The attacker can intercept and modify the vote message without detection.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure communication channels (e.g., TLS) and message integrity checks.

description: An attacker could alter a legitimate vote to change the outcome of the proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of non-repudiation mechanism for votes.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not prevent users from denying their votes.

assumptions: The system lacks a mechanism to verify the authenticity and origin of votes.
The voting mechanism does not incorporate strong non-repudiation features.

mitigation.strategy: Implement digital signatures or other non-repudiation mechanisms for votes.

description: A user could deny having cast a particular vote, potentially leading to disputes or manipulation of governance decisions.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote details might be disclosed to unauthorized actors.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not explicitly protect vote confidentiality.

assumptions: The governance contract does not encrypt or anonymize vote data.
The attacker can access the blockchain or contract storage to retrieve vote details.

mitigation.strategy: Encrypt vote data and implement access controls to limit visibility.

description: An attacker could obtain information about users' votes, potentially compromising their privacy or influencing future voting behavior.

security_gap.gap_description: The system may be vulnerable to flooding attacks.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system might lack rate limiting or other protections against DoS attacks.

assumptions: The governance contract does not have rate limiting or other DoS mitigation mechanisms.
The actor can submit a large number of votes within a short timeframe.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting and other DoS prevention measures.

description: An attacker may flood the governance contract with a high volume of votes, rendering the system unusable.
